@block.description: Generic fallback NOUN for a board item whose title is unknown (a service / module / task node). Not the verb 'to block'.

@close.description: Verb meaning dismiss / shut (a panel, drawer, dialog), NOT the adjective 'near'. Used as an aria-label on close affordances - translate as the imperative verb.

@clear.description: Verb meaning empty / reset a stored value (a config, a saved connection), NOT the adjective 'transparent / obvious'. Used as a destructive-action button label.

@sandbox.description: Named feature area (a screen for trying prompt versions / models against graded fixtures). Fine to localize descriptively per locale - unlike nav.kaizen, this one is NOT kept verbatim.

@kaizen.description: Proper-noun product feature name (grading history + verified prompt/agent/model combos). Do NOT translate - keep 'Kaizen' verbatim in every locale.

@decisionWord.description: Count-based plural noun rendered AFTER a number, e.g. '3 decisions' (pending human decisions in the run queue). Resolved via t(key, count); count is always 1 or more. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - and rely on the custom pluralRules wired in i18n.config.ts).

UMBRELLA over two cases not visible from the string: (a) the service has no test infra configured, AND (b) this deployment's container runtime can't run local test infra (limited mode). The detailed description carries the specific cause; keep the title broad ('not configured / unavailable').

binaryStorageUnconfigured.title: No storage for this pipeline
binaryStorageUnconfigured.body: This pipeline includes an agent that needs binary storage (the UI Tester uploads its screenshots), but this account has no content storage configured. Configure content storage to run it.

personalSubscriptions.heading: Personal subscriptions (individual use only)
personalSubscriptions.intro: Claude (Pro/Max), GLM (Z.ai Coding Plan) and ChatGPT (Codex) are each licensed to you as an individual, so they are stored just for you and only your runs use them. Each token is encrypted under a personal password (never stored) that you enter when you start such a run; it is cached in this browser for a while so it stays out of your way. The password is a low-friction convenience and a transparency signal that the system will not silently share your credential, not a wall against a system-key holder. These models cannot be used on recurring schedules.

renewal.expired: Your {vendor} subscription has expired. Renew it and reconnect to keep running its models.
renewal.soon: Your {vendor} subscription renews in {count} day. Update it here once renewed. | Your {vendor} subscription renews in {count} days. Update it here once renewed.

@soon.description: Count-based renewal reminder; {count} is the number of days until renewal (always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts). {vendor} is the subscription's product name, kept verbatim.

claude.label: Claude (Pro/Max)
claude.step1: Install Claude Code and sign in with your Claude Pro/Max account: run `claude` once and complete the browser login.
claude.step2: Generate a long-lived token: run `claude setup-token` and copy it.
claude.step3: Paste it below and choose a personal password to protect it.

glm.label: GLM (Z.ai Coding Plan)
glm.tokenPlaceholder: your GLM Coding Plan API key
glm.step1: Open your Z.ai GLM Coding Plan dashboard and create an API key for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
glm.step2: The GLM Coding Plan is licensed to you as an individual, so it is stored per-user here (not pooled).
glm.step3: Paste the key below and choose a personal password to protect it.

codex.label: ChatGPT (Codex)
codex.step1: Install the Codex CLI and sign in with your ChatGPT account: run `codex login` and complete the browser flow.
codex.step2: Open the credentials file Codex wrote at ~/.codex/auth.json (on Windows %USERPROFILE%\.codex\auth.json).
codex.step3: Copy the entire contents of auth.json, paste it below, and choose a personal password to protect it.

epicChildren.title: Epic tasks
epicChildren.hint: All tasks assigned to this epic, grouped by the service and module they live in. An epic only groups tasks; it does not run anything itself.

recurring.onDemandHint: Manual only. Runs when you trigger it, and may use an individual-usage subscription model.

fragments.serviceHint: Programming standards for the whole service. The content of each selected fragment is added to the prompt of every code-aware agent working on tasks under this service.
fragments.serviceEmpty: None. Code-aware agents on this service follow their default guidance.

frontendConfig.title: Frontend
frontendConfig.hint: How to build, serve, and mock this frontend for a self-contained UI test. Bindings below link it to the backend services it calls.

detect.title: Detect from repo
detect.hint: Read the linked repo and propose a config to review before applying. Nothing is saved until you apply.
detect.error: Couldn't read the repo. Check the frontend is linked to a connected repository.
detect.none: Nothing frontend-shaped was detected. Set the frontend directory (for a monorepo) or fill the fields in manually.

frontendConfig.directoryHint: The frontend app's subfolder in the repo (a monorepo, e.g. frontend/). Leave empty if the app is at the repo root.
frontendConfig.serveStaticDesc: serves the built output directory as static files. Cheapest for a UI test of a fully built app.
frontendConfig.serveCommandDesc: runs a package.json script (e.g. preview) to serve the app, for builds that need a running server.
frontendConfig.serveEnvAxisNote: Separate from Env injection (build-time vs runtime) below, which controls how backend URLs reach the app, not how it is served.
frontendConfig.mockMappingsHint: WireMock's root directory. Put stub JSON in a mappings/ subfolder inside it (and response bodies in __files/), e.g. mocks/mappings/. A folder with no mappings/ inside runs an empty mock.
frontendConfig.envInjectionHint: Build-time injects the backend URLs as build env vars; Runtime writes them into a window.env shim. Note your build tool may only expose prefixed vars (e.g. VITE_) to the browser, so name bindings to match its convention.
frontendConfig.previewHint: Keeps a served preview running with a clickable URL. Local and Node runtimes only.
frontendConfig.previewUnsupported: This deployment can't host a browsable preview. Run on a Local or Node deployment to keep a served preview with a clickable URL.

bindings.title: Backend bindings
bindings.hint: Each env var the frontend reads for an upstream URL, and where it resolves: a bound service's ephemeral environment, or a WireMock stub.
bindings.empty: No backend bindings. Add one to point an env var at a service or a mock.

resolved.title: Resolves to
resolved.duplicateWarning: Used on more than one binding, so only the last applies: {vars}.

serviceConnections.title: Service connections
serviceConnections.hint: The other services this one uses, e.g. a service that sends its emails. Connections draw edges on the board, and tasks can mark a connected service as directly involved.
serviceConnections.descriptionPlaceholder: How this service uses it, e.g. sends emails via it
serviceConnections.empty: No connections. Add one to link this service to another service it uses.

releaseHealth.title: Post-release health
releaseHealth.sectionHint: After this service's changes merge and ship, a gate watches the mapped monitors and SLOs for a while. On a regression it starts an on-call agent to investigate; nothing is reverted automatically.
releaseHealth.connectPrompt: Connect an observability integration to watch this service after releases.
releaseHealth.hint: The monitors and SLOs the gate watches after this service ships. Comma-separate ids.

testConfig.title: Test infrastructure
testConfig.hint: How a test environment is stood up for this service when a pipeline needs to run it: no infrastructure, a Docker Compose file, Kubernetes manifests, or a custom manifest type.
testConfig.provisionTypeHint: How this service stands up its environment for the Tester. The workspace configures how each type is handled (the engine + connection).
testConfig.browseRepo: Browse the repository for the compose file
testConfig.composeHint: Used by the Tester to stand up the service's dependencies locally.
testConfig.selectComposeHint: Pick the compose file in the repository. Its path is stored relative to the repo root.
testConfig.selectManifestHint: Pick the manifests file or directory in the repository. Its path is stored relative to the repo root.
testConfig.provisioningHint: A hint for provisioning this service's ephemeral test environment: which cloud provider to deploy to and how large an instance to request. Ignored for local (docker-compose) testing.
testConfig.manifestPathHint: File or directory of the per-PR Kubernetes manifests, relative to the repo root.
testConfig.rendererHint: Raw applies the manifests as-is. Kustomize builds an overlay first (needs the container deploy adapter).
testConfig.customNoTypes: No custom manifest types are defined yet. Add one in the Infrastructure window.
testConfig.customManifestIdHint: The custom type this service produces, matched to a remote-custom handler the workspace configures.
testConfig.customManifestPathHint: Prefilled from the type's default when you select it. Use Detect to locate an existing manifest in the repo.

runSettings.hint: How this task's pipeline runs: which pipeline starts, which merge and model policies apply, and what happens around the run.

runSettings.mergePresetEmpty: No preset configured. The merger raises a review notification for every PR.
runSettings.mergePolicyHint: Decides when a finished pull request merges automatically instead of waiting for a human review, and how many CI-fix attempts a run gets.
runSettings.modelPresetHint: Decides which AI model each agent step runs on.
runSettings.modelPresetEmpty: No preset configured. Agents run on the deployment's default routing.
runSettings.unavailableModels: Not available under the current configuration: {models}. This task would fail on those steps.

technicalHint.technical: Technical: the implementer treats the task definition as primary and the spec as a regression reference; the spec-writer may produce no business specs.
technicalHint.business: Business: the specification leads, as usual.
technicalHint.unset: Auto-detect: inferred from the spec phase. Set it explicitly to override.

runSettings.writebackHint: Writes back to this task's linked tracker issue. Overrides the workspace default.
runSettings.responsibleEmpty: Unassigned. Set a product owner to notify them when requirement review flags this task.
runSettings.autoStartHint: When this task merges, automatically start the tasks that depend on it (once their other dependencies are also done).

mergerResult.title: Merge decision
mergerResult.description: How the PR scored and what the engine decided.

reason.within_thresholds: Every score is within the {preset} thresholds, so the PR was merged automatically.
reason.exceeded_thresholds: {axes} exceeded the {preset} thresholds, so the PR is waiting for a human to merge.
reason.auto_merge_disabled: The {preset} preset sends every PR to a human, so this one is waiting for review.
reason.no_rationale: The merger scored the PR but gave no rationale, so the verdict could not be trusted to auto-merge; the PR is waiting for a human to merge.
reason.no_assessment: The merger did not return a parseable assessment, so the PR is waiting for a human to merge.
reason.merge_failed: The scores were within the {preset} thresholds, but the automatic merge could not complete (for example branch protection or a conflict), so the PR is waiting for a human to merge.
reason.merge_partial: Some of the task's pull requests merged, but a later one could not, so the change is waiting for a human to finish or revert the multi-repo merge.

mergerResult.noAssessment: The merger did not return a scored assessment.

executionBackend.label: Where agents run
executionBackend.local-dockerDesc: Run each agent in a container on this machine's Docker daemon.
executionBackend.cloudflare-containersDesc: Run agents on Cloudflare's built-in per-run container platform.
executionBackend.kubernetesDesc: Run each agent as a pod in a Kubernetes cluster you operate.
executionBackend.runner-poolDesc: Dispatch agents to your own scheduler behind an HTTP manifest API.
executionBackend.k3sDesc: Prefilled for a local k3s/k3d cluster on this machine — just paste a token.

testEnvBackend.label: Where test environments run
testEnvBackend.local-composeDesc: Stand the Tester's dependencies up with docker-compose inside the run's container. For a full app preview env, use the Docker Compose preview env option below.
testEnvBackend.kubernetesDesc: Provision a per-PR namespace in a Kubernetes cluster you operate. Best for Kubernetes-native apps or when you already run a cluster.
testEnvBackend.environment-providerDesc: Provision ephemeral environments through your own HTTP management API. Best when you already have bespoke preview-env tooling.
testEnvBackend.composeDesc: Stand the repo's own docker-compose.yml up on a local Docker daemon as the Tester's URL. Best for local Compose-based apps. Supports pre-built images or building from source; needs a Docker daemon, so local deployments only.

infrastructure.dockerComposeInfo: Docker Compose services stand up on the runtime's local Docker: no connection needed (the run's container must support Docker-in-Docker). Configure the service, port, and image source (pull pre-built or build from source) on the service's environment settings.

kubernetesEngine.localK3sHint: Prefilled for a local k3s/k3d/kind cluster on this machine. Bind a ServiceAccount to a role, mint its token with `kubectl create token NAME -n NAMESPACE` (Kubernetes 1.24+), and paste it below. Then choose how the environment URL is derived, and edit the API server URL if your cluster listens on a different port.

autoSetup.title: Auto-setup with the CLI
autoSetup.description: Run this in your terminal to probe or provision a local cluster, mint a ServiceAccount token, and open this form pre-filled. Paste the token it prints, then Test and Save.

kubernetesEngine.label: Connection label
kubernetesEngine.apiTokenHelp: Write-only; re-enter it to rotate. Stored encrypted, never returned.
kubernetesEngine.apiTokenHelpStored: A token is saved and reused for tests. Leave blank to keep it, or enter a new one to replace it.
kubernetesEngine.apiTokenPlaceholderStored: Leave blank to keep the saved token
kubernetesEngine.hostTemplateHelp: The host template, rendered with the provision vars (e.g. branch, sha).
kubernetesEngine.namespaceTemplateHelp: The per-PR namespace name, rendered with the provision vars. For kustomize, leave empty to honor the overlay's namespace.
kubernetesEngine.imageTemplateHelp: An image reference made available to the manifests as the image variable.
kubernetesEngine.caCertPemHelp: Verifies the apiserver TLS cert. Omit only for a publicly-trusted CA.

customType.title: Custom manifest types
customType.hint: The open catalog of custom provision types a service can pin. Add your own below; types from code are read-only.
customType.label: Label
customType.acceptsInputHintHelp: Describes the input shape the provider expects.
customType.defaultManifestPathHelp: Prefilled on a service that pins this type, and the seed for path auto-detection. A complete path (deploy/preview.yaml) or a bare filename.
customType.fixerPromptHelp: Coding-agent instructions to generate or fix the manifest. When set, a service shows a Generate / fix button.
customType.saveFailed: Could not save the custom type
customType.removeFailed: Could not remove the custom type

handler.intro: Configure how each provision type a service declares is handled: the engine and connection. A service's manifests/compose path (the what/where) is set on the service.
handler.customBackendHelp: Which registered backend provisions this custom environment.
handler.personalOverrideHint: Override the workspace handler for this provision type on your own machine. Stored per user.

providerConnection.noneAvailable: No infrastructure providers are enabled on this deployment.

environment.title: Ephemeral environment provider
environment.blurb: Where the Tester agent runs against a live preview environment. Configure the per-workspace settings and credentials your provider needs.

runner-pool.title: Agent runner backend
runner-pool.blurb: Where the coding agents run when not using Cloudflare Containers. Choose a self-hosted runner pool (your own scheduler) or a Kubernetes cluster, then configure its endpoint and credentials.

kubernetesEnv.label: Name
kubernetesEnv.apiServerUrlHelp: The kube-apiserver root, e.g. https://10.0.0.1:6443. The orchestrator applies each PR's manifests through the apiserver, so only this endpoint must be reachable.
kubernetesEnv.apiTokenHelp: A bearer token with RBAC to create namespaces and apply the operator's resources. Stored encrypted; never shown again.
kubernetesEnv.repoHelp: The owner/repo that holds the Kubernetes manifests, e.g. acme/preview-manifests.
kubernetesEnv.refHelp: Branch, tag or SHA to read the manifests at. Empty uses the repo's default branch.
kubernetesEnv.pathHelp: File or directory within the repo holding the resources to apply.
kubernetesEnv.hostTemplateHelp: Host template rendered with the provision vars such as branch and pullNumber; the rendered host becomes the environment URL.
kubernetesEnv.ingressNameHelp: Ingress to read the load-balancer host from. Empty uses the only Ingress applied.
kubernetesEnv.namespaceTemplateHelp: Per-PR namespace name rendered from the provision vars such as pullNumber. Empty derives one from the PR number.
kubernetesEnv.imageTemplateHelp: Image reference exposed to the manifests, rendered over the provision vars such as branch and sha.
kubernetesEnv.caCertPemHelp: Paste the cluster CA bundle so the apiserver's TLS certificate verifies. Omit only for a publicly-trusted CA.
kubernetesEnv.insecureSkipTlsVerifyHelp: Strongly discouraged. Disables apiserver TLS verification; use only for kind/dev clusters.
kubernetesEnv.invalidFields: Complete the required fields with valid values to connect.

kubernetes.label: Name
kubernetes.apiServerUrlHelp: The kube-apiserver root, e.g. https://10.0.0.1:6443. The orchestrator reaches each run's pod through the apiserver, so only this endpoint must be reachable.
kubernetes.imageHelp: The executor-harness image each per-run pod runs.
kubernetes.apiTokenHelp: A bearer token with RBAC to create, get and delete pods and pods/proxy in the namespace. Stored encrypted; never shown again.
kubernetes.caCertPemHelp: Paste the cluster CA bundle so the apiserver's TLS certificate verifies. Omit only for a publicly-trusted CA.
kubernetes.insecureSkipTlsVerifyHelp: Strongly discouraged. Disables apiserver TLS verification; use only for local k3s/kind/dev clusters.

manifestEditor.title: Provider manifest
manifestEditor.jsonHelp: Describe your provider's API declaratively: the base URL, auth scheme, request templates, and the response mapping. Validated against the same contract the server enforces.
manifestEditor.invalidJson: The manifest is not valid JSON.
manifestEditor.invalidShape: The manifest does not match the expected shape.
manifestEditor.reenterSecrets: Re-enter every secret to save. Stored secrets are write-only and aren't shown.
manifestEditor.starterHint: This is a starter example. Edit it to match your provider's API.

form.reenterSecrets: Re-enter the secret field to save changes — stored secrets are write-only and aren't shown. | Re-enter the secret fields to save changes — stored secrets are write-only and aren't shown.

@reenterSecrets.description: Count-based: how many write-only secret fields the user must re-supply before re-saving (count is always >= 1). Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

form.missingFields: Fill in the required fields to connect: {fields}.

serviceFragmentDefaults.intro: Pick the best-practice fragments every new service starts with. Their guidance is folded into the prompt of every code-aware agent (coder, reviewer, architect, fixers) on the service's tasks. You can refine the set per service in its inspector; changing this default does not affect services that already exist.
serviceFragmentDefaults.empty: None — new services start with no service-level fragments.

footer.question: Need to add or edit the fragments themselves (hand-authored, linked docs, repos)?

filing.heading: Where tickets are filed
filing.description: The tech-debt recurring pipeline raises an issue before implementation and files it in this tracker. Choose {none} to skip filing.
filing.githubHint: GitHub Issues rides your installed GitHub App, which isn't connected yet. Install it under {link} — filing stays off until then.
filing.jiraHint: Jira isn't connected yet. {link} to file and link issues.
filing.linearHint: Linear isn't connected yet. {link} to file and link issues.
filing.jiraProjectKeyHelp: New tickets are filed under this project.
filing.linearTeamIdHelp: New issues are created under this team (Linear requires a team to create an issue).

linking.heading: Link issues as context
linking.description: When a source is offered you can import its issues and attach them to a task, so agents see the issue description and comments while implementing. This is independent of the filing tracker above.

github.available: Rides your installed GitHub App — no credentials needed.
github.unavailable: Install the GitHub App (Integrations → GitHub) to offer this source.

jira.connectHint: Connect with a Jira account and API token.

linear.connectHint: Connect with a Linear personal API key.

writeback.heading: Writeback
writeback.description: Write back to a task's linked issue(s) as its pull request progresses. Each toggle is the workspace default and can be overridden per task in the inspector. GitHub issues close natively; Jira issues transition to the first status in their {done} category.

commentOnOpen.label: Comment when a PR opens
commentOnOpen.help: Post a comment on the linked issue with the new pull request's link.

resolveOnMerge.label: Close as resolved when a PR merges
resolveOnMerge.help: Comment that the PR merged, then close / resolve the linked issue.

probeFailure.title: Issue tracking isn't available
probeFailure.disabled: The task-source integration is turned off on this deployment (its encryption key is not configured). Set ENCRYPTION_KEY on the backend to enable issue tracking.
probeFailure.serverError: The issue-tracker service returned an error (HTTP {status}): {message}. This usually means the backend isn't fully migrated/configured.

userSecrets.title: My GitHub token
userSecrets.intro: Store a personal access token used for the runs {you} start — pushes, pull requests, the CI gate and merges are attributed to your GitHub access. Stored {scope}; the token is write-only and never shown again.

confirmRemove.title: Remove this secret?
confirmRemove.body: "{name}" will be removed. This can't be undone.

mergeThresholds.intro: Named auto-merge policies a task can choose. After CI passes, the {merger} agent scores the PR on complexity, risk and impact (0-100%); the PR auto-merges only when every score is at or below the preset's ceilings, otherwise a review notification is raised. The default preset governs any task that picks none.

confirmDelete.title: Delete this merge preset?
confirmDelete.body: "{name}" will be removed. Tasks using it fall back to the workspace default.

mergeThresholds.deleteDefaultBlocked: The default preset cannot be deleted
mergeThresholds.autoMergeOffHint: Always route the PR to a human review.

observabilityConnection.title: Post-release health
observabilityConnection.intro: After a release ships, the {gate} gate watches the configured observability monitors/SLOs. On a regression it spawns an on-call agent to investigate (a human decides whether to revert). Map which monitors/SLOs a service watches from that service's inspector.

incident.heading: Incident enrichment
incident.description: Optional. On a regression, the on-call investigation is posted onto an incident these systems ALREADY opened from the same signals (annotate, never re-alert). Secrets are write-only: blank leaves a stored value unchanged.

packageRegistries.title: Private package registries
packageRegistries.intro: Connect the private registries your repositories install from. Agents receive them when they check out a repository, so private dependencies resolve during installs. Tokens are write-only: to change one, remove the entry and add it again.

add.heading: Add a registry
add.host: The token is sent only to {host}.

toast.removeFailed: Could not remove the registry entry

sharedStacks.intro: Long-lived compose infrastructure (databases, brokers, search, mail) brought up once per workspace and reused across runs and pull requests. A test environment attaches to a stack's managed network. Bringing a stack up runs on a local Docker deployment; on other backends you can still manage the definition.

add.heading: Add a shared stack
add.name: Name
add.cloneUrlHelp: The git repository the stack's compose files live in.
add.managedNetworksHelp: Networks this stack creates and owns for consumers to attach to.

toast.createFailed: Could not add the shared stack
toast.updateFailed: Could not update the shared stack
toast.startFailed: Could not start the shared stack
toast.stopFailed: Could not stop the shared stack
toast.removeFailed: Could not delete the shared stack

localMode.title: Local mode
localMode.intro: Tuning for the local container runner, stored on this machine's deployment (it replaced the {poolVars} / {harnessVars} env vars). Saving resizes the warm pool live, no restart needed; in-flight runs keep the container they already hold.

pool.heading: Warm container pool
pool.description: Keep idle harness containers ready and re-lease one (preferring a container that already holds the run's repo) instead of cold-starting per run. Pool size 0 disables it. Requires a Docker-family runtime (Docker/Podman/OrbStack/Colima); ignored on Apple {appleContainer}.

size.label: Pool size
size.help: Max idle warm containers. 0 = pooling off.

minWarm.label: Pre-warm at boot
minWarm.help: Containers started when the service boots.

max.label: Max containers
max.help: Hard cap (leased + idle). Blank = pool size.

idleTimeout.label: Idle timeout (minutes)
idleTimeout.help: Evict an idle pooled container after.

checkout.heading: Checkout reuse
checkout.description: When a warm container already holds the run's repo, the harness reuses its per-repo checkout (clean sweep + fetch + switch branch) instead of cloning fresh.

workspaceRoot.label: Workspace root
workspaceRoot.help: Absolute in-container directory the reused checkout lives under.

cleanKeep.label: Keep on clean (comma-separated)
cleanKeep.help: Dependency-cache directories the per-run clean sweep preserves.

openRouterCatalog.intro: Reach {models} through one gateway. Add your OpenRouter key below, then enable the models you want. They appear in the model picker with their context window and price, and meter against your spend budget.
openRouterCatalog.step1: Open {link} and create an API key.
openRouterCatalog.step2: Copy the key (starts with {prefix}) and paste it below.
openRouterCatalog.empty: No models yet. Hit {action} to load OpenRouter's live list.

waiting.heading: Waiting for a human
waiting.body: A run parked on a human decision (a review, an approval, a merge) waits as long as it needs and is never cancelled. After this many minutes its notification turns red and is flagged {overdue} in the inbox.

taskLimit.heading: Running tasks per service
taskLimit.body: Cap how many tasks may run at once under one service. Starting a task over the limit is refused with a clear message until a running task finishes.

observability.heading: Agent observability
observability.body: Store the complete context provided to each agent: the composed prompts, the best-practice fragments folded in, and the full content of the files injected into its container, so it can be inspected later in the observability view. The bodies are kept for the same window as the per-call LLM telemetry. Turn off to stop storing it (existing snapshots are pruned by retention).

retention.heading: Screenshot retention
retention.body: How long to keep the UI tester's captured screenshots and the reference design images they're reviewed against (the visual-confirmation gate). A daily cleanup job deletes both the image bytes and their metadata once they age past this window.

kaizen.heading: Kaizen agent
kaizen.body: After each run completes, the Kaizen agent grades how every agent step went, from smooth and efficient to confused and chaotic, and recommends prompt/model improvements. A prompt + agent + model combination that grades highly with no recommendations five times in a row is marked verified and is no longer graded. Grading runs in the background and is shown inside run details and the Kaizen screen. Set the grader's model in Model Configuration (the "Kaizen" agent).

@body.description: "Kaizen" is a product feature name; keep it verbatim in every locale.

budget.heading: Monthly spend budget
budget.body: Token usage is metered per LLM call, priced, and gated by these budgets. A run pauses when any tier it belongs to is exhausted. Leave a field blank for no limit on that tier (the workspace tier then inherits the built-in default, about 100 EUR/month).
budget.accountBody: A ceiling across every workspace in this account.
budget.userBody: A ceiling across every run you start, in any workspace.
budget.hardCapHint: set by the deployment; the value can't exceed it
budget.adminOnly: Only an account admin can change the account budget.

localModelEndpoints.title: My local runners
localModelEndpoints.intro: Point agents at an LLM running on {ownMachine} (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible server). A runner is stored {justForYou} (a runner lives on your box), and the models you enable appear automatically in the model picker. The API key (most runners ignore it) is write-only and never shown again.
localModelEndpoints.apiKeyKeepPlaceholder: leave blank to keep stored key
localModelEndpoints.reachable: Reachable · no models | Reachable · {count} model | Reachable · {count} models

confirmRemove.title: Remove this runner?
confirmRemove.body: "{name}" will be removed. This can't be undone.

presetMismatch.title: Preset uses unavailable models
presetMismatch.intro: The workspace default model preset "{name}" assigns models that aren't available under the current configuration. Tasks that use this preset would fail when they reach those steps.
presetMismatch.advice: Either repoint the preset at models you have configured, or add the missing provider.

onboarding.title: Set up an AI model provider
onboarding.noModelTitle: No AI model is available on this workspace yet.
onboarding.noModelBody: Agents need a model to run. AI works out of the box only on a Cloudflare deployment with Workers AI enabled; otherwise connect at least one source below.
onboarding.operatorNote: AWS Bedrock and Cloudflare Workers AI are enabled by the deployment operator via environment configuration, not from this screen.

keys.title: Provider keys & subscriptions
keys.body: Add a direct provider API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Qwen, …) or connect a commercial coding-plan subscription (Kimi, DeepSeek) or a personal one (Claude, GLM, Codex).

openrouter.title: OpenRouter gateway
openrouter.body: Enable models through the OpenRouter gateway with a single key. Browse and turn on the models you want.

local.title: My local runners
local.body: Point cat-factory at a model you run yourself (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, …). No API key, no spend.

personalCredential.connectBody: This task uses a {vendor} model, which runs on your own {vendor} subscription. Connect (or renew) it first, then start the run again.
personalCredential.passwordBody: Enter the personal password that protects your {vendor} subscription. It unlocks your credential for this run only and is cached in this browser so you won't be asked again for a while.

apiKeys.directAccountIntro: Connect a vendor API key shared by every workspace in this account. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Account keys are admin-managed.
apiKeys.directIntro: Connect a vendor API key so models run directly on that provider. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Scope a key to this workspace (shared with the team) or to you (your own pool, usable anywhere).
apiKeys.proxyAccountIntro: Connect a proxy key (OpenRouter, LiteLLM) shared by every workspace in this account. A proxy is an intermediary that fronts many vendors behind a single key. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage.
apiKeys.proxyIntro: Connect a proxy key (OpenRouter, LiteLLM). A proxy is an intermediary that reaches many vendors' models through a single gateway, rather than one vendor directly. Keys are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Scope a key to this workspace (shared with the team) or to you (your own pool, usable anywhere).
apiKeys.cachingNote: Enables prompt caching for {provider} models, so a long multi-turn run reuses its cached prompt prefix instead of re-sending it every turn.
apiKeys.usage: {tokens} tok this window · {count} call | {tokens} tok this window · {count} calls

@usage.description: Usage readout for a connected API key. {tokens} is a preformatted token count; {count} is the number of API calls in the window and drives the plural. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

openai.label: OpenAI
openai.step1: Open platform.openai.com → API keys and create a new secret key.
openai.step2: Copy the key (starts with sk-…); it is shown only once.

anthropic.label: Anthropic (Claude API)
anthropic.step1: Open console.anthropic.com → Settings → API Keys and create a key.

qwen.label: Qwen (Alibaba DashScope)
qwen.step1: Open the DashScope console (international) → API-KEY and create a key.
qwen.step2: Copy the key; it authenticates the OpenAI-compatible Qwen endpoint.

deepseek.label: DeepSeek
deepseek.step1: Open platform.deepseek.com → API keys and create a key.

moonshot.label: Moonshot (Kimi API)
moonshot.step1: Open platform.moonshot.ai → API Keys and create a key.
moonshot.step2: Copy the key; it authenticates the OpenAI-compatible Moonshot endpoint.

openrouter.label: OpenRouter
openrouter.step1: Open openrouter.ai → Keys and create an API key.
openrouter.step2: Copy the key (starts with sk-or-…); it reaches 300+ models through one gateway.

litellm.label: LiteLLM (self-hosted gateway)
litellm.step1: Generate a virtual key on your LiteLLM gateway (or use its master key).
litellm.step2: The gateway's base URL is set by your deployment operator (LITELLM_BASE_URL), not here.

vendorCredentials.title: LLM Vendors
vendorCredentials.poolIntro: Connect a commercial coding-plan subscription (Kimi, DeepSeek) that permits team/organization use to run agent steps on the Claude Code harness instead of an API key. Tokens are stored encrypted, pooled, and rotated by usage. Subscription models are flat-rate quota, so they don't draw on your spend budget. Individual-use subscriptions (Claude, GLM, ChatGPT/Codex) are connected per-user in the Personal subscriptions tab.
vendorCredentials.usage: {tokens} tok this window · {count} run | {tokens} tok this window · {count} runs

@usage.description: Usage readout for a connected subscription token. {tokens} is a preformatted token count; {count} is the number of runs in the window and drives the plural. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - via the custom pluralRules in i18n.config.ts).

kimi.label: Kimi (Moonshot coding plan)
kimi.step1: Open your Moonshot (Kimi) coding-plan console and create an API key for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
kimi.step2: Copy the API key. Agent steps will run via Claude Code against Moonshot's Anthropic endpoint with full context.

deepseek.label: DeepSeek (coding plan)
deepseek.step1: Open your DeepSeek coding-plan console and create an API key for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint.
deepseek.step2: Copy the API key. Agent steps will run via Claude Code against DeepSeek's Anthropic endpoint with full context.

confirmRemove.title: Remove this credential?
confirmRemove.body: "{name}" will be removed. This can't be undone.

onboarding.title: Connect cat-factory to GitHub
onboarding.intro: cat-factory works by opening pull requests on your repositories. Install the GitHub App on your account or organization to continue, and you can grant it every repository or pick a subset.

connect.noInstallations: No installations found for the App yet. Install it below, or connect by ID.

errors.startInstall: Could not start the GitHub App install

panel.connectIntro: Connect a GitHub App installation to back board blocks with repositories, browse pull requests and issues, and let agents push branches and open PRs.
panel.manageHint: Pick the repositories this board should track. The GitHub connection is shared across the account, and each board links its own repos.
panel.noAvailableRepos: The installation can't access any repositories yet.
panel.noLinkedRepos: No repositories linked yet. Use Manage repos to pick which repositories this board tracks.

confirmDisconnect.title: Disconnect GitHub?
confirmDisconnect.body: The app will lose access to your repositories until you reconnect.

addService.title: Add a service from a repository
addService.intro: Pick an existing GitHub repository to add as a board service. No bootstrapping: the repo is linked to a new service frame as-is, and tasks you run on it target that repo.
addService.connectFirst: Connect this workspace to GitHub first. Link an installation the App is already on, or install it.
addService.repositoryHint: Repositories the GitHub App can access. Don't see yours? Grant the App access below, then refresh.
addService.repoTypeHint: What this repo is: a backend service, a frontend app, a shared library, or a document repository (docs/spikes only).
addService.noReposAvailable: No repositories available yet. Grant the App access to one below, then refresh.
addService.searchPlaceholder: Search repositories by owner or name…

@searchMinChars.description: Shown when a large repo list needs a typed query before searching. Resolved via t(key, { min }, min) so {min} also drives the plural choice; min is always 2 or more. Provide ALL plural forms your language needs (English has 2; Polish/Ukrainian need 3 - one/few/many - and rely on the custom pluralRules wired in i18n.config.ts).

addService.monorepoLabel: This is a monorepo (hosts more than one service)
addService.monorepoDescription: Add several services from one repo, each pinned to a subdirectory.
addService.monorepoBrowseHint: Browse the repository and pick the directory of the service you want to add. Agents working on this service will run within that subdirectory.
addService.grantAccess: Grant the App access to a repo
addService.grantAccessTitle: Open the App's installation settings to grant it access to a repository
